observations: Lines 3-15 keep the action baseline tight and verb-forward; the opening sequence earns its clipped compression.
Lines 33-61 collapse into short-turn banter plus one-line narration, pushing the chapter into a rhythm flatline instead of the guide's mixed 10-12 word cadence.
Lines 97-145 carry the runner report in stacked short declaratives; the urgency works, but the connective tissue stays well below target.
Lines 181-227 keep the ride south readable and close, yet the sentence chain remains too choppy for a travel-and-briefing stretch.
Lines 249-276 render Cyndia's report in useful field language, but the passage still overuses hard stops where compounds or participial links should carry pressure.
Lines 381-399 land the holding-room reveal with strong scene pressure, though the cadence is still dominated by short declaratives rather than earned contrast.
Lines 5-9 stay in Sylvanas's tactical field vocabulary: footing, range, stride, and the bow-work all read profession-first.
Lines 27-31 ground the patrol world in road mud, ward hum, and runestones without drifting into document voice or decorative fantasy diction.
Lines 105-139 keep 'rot' and 'lines' plain in dialogue; no one starts speaking like a memo or a lab report.
Lines 211-219 render Jaina through field markers—ink, frost burns, split hands—rather than abstract scholarly labeling.
The banned-term sweep found no hits for actionable, vector, optimal, substrate, propagation, algorithm, data, dynamics, or similar report-language contamination.
Metaphor density stays low across the chapter, comfortably inside the 1-3 per thousand rule.
Lines 29-31 rely on concrete border imagery rather than ornamental comparison; the road and the chamber do real story work.
Lines 137-139 use spoiled grain and wells as Sylvanas's language for evaluating 'rot,' which fits the world's practical register.
Line 395 uses 'like a second accusation' as a mild comparison; it functions, but it is less Sylvanas-coded than the stronger ranger or tactical images elsewhere.
No forbidden storm, drowning, shattered, burden, or wall/armor/mask emotional metaphors survived into v4b.
The chapter contains 200 paragraphs, with the rhythm scorer measuring 59.8% one-sentence paragraphs against a target range of 15-24%.
Paragraph run P17-P31 (lines 33-61) overuses isolated dialogue turns and single-line beats, flattening the page shape.
Paragraph run P49-P73 (lines 97-145) keeps tension high but still reads as a stacked sequence of short blocks rather than varied procedural prose.
Paragraph run P125-P138 (lines 249-276) compresses report, reaction, and inference into too many one-sentence units.
The chapter does avoid the opposite failure: there are no overlong 100+ word narration blocks clogging the read.
The rough dialogue mix lands at about 36:64 dialogue-to-narration words, more narration-heavy than the procedural-chapter target of 60:40 dialogue-to-narration.
Attribution balance is off: rough pass found about 16.4% said/asked, 14.1% action/context attribution, and 69.5% untagged dialogue paragraphs.
The longest untagged run exceeds the guide's four-line ceiling, especially in the Lor'themar banter around lines 35-61.
Lines 101-145 remain clear despite sparse tags because speaker identity is easy to track, so clarity is not the issue; distribution is.
Creative tag policy passes cleanly: no murmured, breathed, intoned, purred, or adverb-loaded tags appear in the chapter.
Lines 3-15 demonstrate Sylvanas's auditory-spatial filter: threat, footing, range, and movement arrive before beauty or self-reflection.
Lines 63-65 keep her road-thoughts territorial and bodily rather than reflective; south suits her because it lets her breathe.
Lines 165-175 keep her humor deadpan and proprietary, especially 'Go be expensive, Lor'themar.'
Line 165 in v4 named an inward satisfaction too abstractly for her calibration; v4b revises it to a steadier physical effect.
Line 359 in v4 named a muted reaction in abstract terms; v4b cuts that to the flatter in-voice judgment 'It landed flat.'
Lines 383 and 387 in v4 leaned too hard into inferred interiority for Jaina; v4b replaces those with visible posture and jaw-set evidence.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas emotionalRegister — action/physical effect instead of abstract emotional naming
fixes.before: Old satisfaction moved through her at the sight of it.
fixes.after: The sight of it steadied her.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas specificRules — no abstract emotional summary when a harder judgment will do
fixes.before: It should have pleased her more than it did.

fixes.rule: POV violation check — replace inferred interior state with visible body language
fixes.before: She held herself braced for disbelief and finished with courtesy about it.
fixes.after: Shoulders set, courtesy still in place.

fixes.rule: POV violation check — render visible pressure instead of claiming hidden feeling
fixes.before: The prisoner met Sylvanas straight on at once, tired at the edges and furious enough to hide it.
fixes.after: The prisoner met Sylvanas straight on at once, tired at the edges, jaw set hard enough to show what courtesy covered.

observations: Rough adverb rate is 14 -ly words across 3022 words, or about 1.16 per 250-word page, which stays below the maxAdverbsPerPage limit of 2.
Rough passive-voice rate is about 2.1%, well inside the 8% ceiling.
The simile cap passes: two 'like' uses and one 'as if' use across the full chapter.
Filter-verb sweep found no narration hits for noticed, observed, realized, registered, processed, catalogued, or assessed.
The prose still misses the quality floor indirectly through telegram texture, but it does not miss the hard numerical floor on adverbs or passive voice.
Exposition integration passes: lines 27-31, 81-85, and 251-267 feed world detail through patrol work, ward handling, and physical evidence rather than narrator aside.
Power dynamics pass: Sylvanas controls sentence pressure and topic control through most exchanges, especially lines 155-175 and 363-399.
Scene shape passes: the chapter opens in action, turns at the rider arrival in lines 91-99, and resolves on the interrogation demand at lines 397-399.
Speculative integration passes the domestication test: wards and leylines are treated as border infrastructure and military problems, not spectacle.
Humor deployment fits the early-arc policy: dry wit remains alive in manageable-threat scenes and contracts as the chapter tightens around the prisoner.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 3-31 and 205-227 stay military-precise, field-practical, and free of modern or clinical diction.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: fail — close-third pressure is right, but chapter-wide cadence stays too short and too fragmented for the style guide's mixed baseline.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — metaphor use remains sparse and mostly concrete, with only a mild drift at line 395.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: mixed — mostly compressed and physical, but v4 needed cleanup at lines 165 and 359 where emotion was summarized too abstractly.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — Sylvanas reads threat, competence, containment, and political consequence as a running tactical brief throughout the chapter.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — sound, position, wind, road condition, and ward-hum dominate her attention ahead of ornamental detail.

ruleViolations: Line 165 (v4): abstract emotional naming softened Sylvanas's register; revised in v4b.
Line 359 (v4): abstract reaction summary weakened the harder Sylvanas cadence; revised in v4b.
Lines 383 and 387 (v4): close-third inference about Jaina's inner state went beyond what Sylvanas could strictly know; revised in v4b.
Chapter-wide: sentence length and paragraph cadence drift too far into telegram territory for Sylvanas POV prose.

flaggedZones.instruction: Join adjacent banter beats with action-led compound sentences and add at least one participial connector in the narration between exchanges.
Blend the runner's report into fewer sentence units using ', and' / ', but' joins and selective em-dash pivots instead of isolated statement bursts.
Enrich the ride-and-briefing stretch with participial phrases that carry motion and speech together rather than stopping after each fact.
Convert the ward-stone report from hard-stop fragments into compound clauses that bind evidence, reaction, and inference in the same sentence.
Break the satchel-and-yard sequence out of its one-line cadence by joining inventory details to Sylvanas's judgments with connective clauses.
In the holding-room reveal, add connective phrasing around the visual inventory so the description lands as one controlled sweep instead of a stop-start scan.

textureFindings.priorityInstruction: Participial density is 1.1% against a 12.0% target — add ', Ving' connective phrases and compound joins in the flagged zones before any finer-grain polish.
